title: Consult ask (the strong brief)
description: A bounded ask an asking agent writes when it hits a goal/intent question it cannot resolve from its own context, or a hard blocker (R7/R14). It is the ONLY channel the question travels upward on: the asker returns a verdict and leaves its full transcript on disk; the answering lead answers from this ask ALONE and never reads the transcript (DI1/R11). The ask is deliberately a strong brief — it carries the asker's recommendation, the alternatives it weighed, evidence lines with concrete file/tool references, a classification proof that this is a goal/intent question (not a local-implementation choice), and what would change the recommendation — so a lead can reject a thin ask rather than answer on a weak record (R14). The transcript_pointer is the U2 capability naming the asker's own transcript for the continuation; it is opaque to the lead.

ask_id.description: Stable id of this ask. Names the artifact file (consults/asks/<ask_id>.json) and is referenced by the answer (consult-answer.ask_id) and by the chain (entry.input_ask_id).

asker_agent_id.description: Spawn-time agent id of the asker. Ties the ask to the chain entry that produced it and to the transcript_pointer that names the asker's own transcript.

logical_unit.description: The logical unit this ask belongs to. One logical unit is a chain of physical children (R23); every ask, answer, and chain entry for that unit shares this value so the unit is reconstructable from files alone.

question.description: The single blocking question, stated plainly. Not a status update — the one thing the asker needs decided to proceed.

recommendation.description: The asker's own recommended answer with its reasoning. A real recommendation, not a shrug: it is what the lead measures the answer against (the decision-changed signal, R29) and what makes a rubber-stamp visible.

alternatives.description: The alternatives the asker considered and why it did not pick them. May be empty only when there is genuinely one defensible option; an empty array on a real fork is a thin ask the lead should reject.

evidence.description: Evidence lines grounding the question and recommendation, each tied to a concrete source. At least one line is required — an ask with no evidence is a thin ask (R14).

file_or_tool.description: The file path or tool/command the evidence comes from (e.g. 'src/auth.ts' or 'npm test').

ref.description: The precise locator within the source — a line range, a symbol name, a test name, a command's output marker.

claim.description: What this evidence establishes, in one line — the inference the asker draws from it.

classification_proof.description: The argument that this is a GOAL/INTENT question eligible for the consult gate (R4), not a local-implementation choice the asker should make itself. Required: a lead rejects an ask that lacks it as reject-as-local (AE2). This is the gate that keeps the asker from over-asking.

would_change.description: What new information or counter-evidence would change the asker's recommendation. Names the decision's sensitivity so the lead knows what to weigh and so a later push-back's 'genuinely new evidence' is judged against a stated baseline (R6).

kind.description: goal-intent: a question eligible for the consult gate (R4). hard-blocker: an ungated blocker that climbs via the existing blocked path (R2) — recorded as an ask for auditability, but it does not require the goal-recheck answer flow.

transcript_pointer.description: The U2 structured-capability pointer naming the ASKER's own transcript, for the continuation to rehydrate from laterally (R15/R17). It is OPAQUE to the lead (R11): the lead passes it through unread into the continuation envelope and never reads the transcript it names.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the ask was written. Orders asks within a logical unit for the chain and the thrash/budget meter (U5).
